2604.11225.title: Sign Language Recognition in the Age of LLMs
2604.11225.paper_summary: Recent Vision Language Models (VLMs) have demonstrated strong performance across a wide range of multimodal reasoning tasks. This raises the question of whether such general-purpose models can also address specialized visual recognition problems such as isolated sign language recognition (ISLR) without task-specific training. In this work, we investigate the capability of modern VLMs to perform ISLR in a zero-shot setting. We evaluate several open-source and proprietary VLMs on the WLASL300 benchmark. Our experiments show that, under prompt-only zero-shot inference, current open-source VLMs remain far behind classic supervised ISLR classifiers by a wide margin. However, follow-up experiments reveal that these models capture partial visual-semantic alignment between signs and text descriptions. Larger proprietary models achieve substantially higher accuracy, highlighting the importance of model scale and training data diversity. All our code is publicly available on GitHub.
2604.11225.paper_summary_zh: 最近的視覺語言模型（VLMs）在多種多模態推理任務中展現了強大的性能。這引發了這樣的問題：這些通用模型是否也能在沒有特定任務訓練的情況下解決專門的視覺識別問題，例如孤立手語識別（ISLR）。在這項工作中，我們調查了現代 VLMs 在零樣本設置中執行 ISLR 的能力。我們在 WLASL300 基準上評估了幾個開源和專有的 VLMs。我們的實驗顯示，在僅使用提示的零樣本推理下，目前的開源 VLMs 在性能上仍然遠遠落後於經典的監督式 ISLR 分類器。然而，後續實驗顯示這些模型能夠捕捉到手勢與文本描述之間的部分視覺-語義對齊。較大的專有模型達到了顯著更高的準確率，突顯了模型規模和訓練數據多樣性的重要性。我們的所有代碼都已公開在 GitHub 上。
2604.11225.author: Vaclav Javorek et.al.
2604.11225.authors: Vaclav Javorek, Jakub Honzik, Ivan Gruber, Tomas Zelezny, Marek Hruz
